preview_text:
The model favors Norway at 64% to win, with Iraq's chances assessed at 12%. The underdog faces a steep task, though the model assigns a meaningful draw probability of 25%, suggesting the match will not necessarily be a runaway.

The win-loss spread is wide, reflecting a clear gap in expected quality. Norway's advantage is substantial, but the 25% draw rate indicates the contest carries enough variance that neither side winning outright is the dominant outcome. A Norway victory remains most likely, yet Iraq retains paths to avoid defeat.

The market is notably more bullish on Norway, pricing the favorite at 75% compared to the model's 64%—a gap of 11 percentage points. The market also compresses the draw to 16%, eight points below the model estimate. This divergence suggests the sportsbooks view Norway's edge as wider and the match as less competitive. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these forecasts.
